commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is needed; the PR is ready for ordinary maintainer review and CI gating.; Cleared: The diff only touches Swift usage-window mapping, pace display gating, and tests; it does not change dependencies, scripts, credential handling, or network permissions. (sha=9b974c5ca306f49ca2fac8dde486cfc9e4be1e90)
commands.review_summary:
The branch assigns explicit 5-hour session and 7-day weekly windows to Ollama, allows Ollama session pace rendering, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main maps Ollama windows with nil durations and rejects `.ollama` in session pace rendering, while the generic weekly pace path requires explicit durations. I did not run the app or tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; the PR is ready for ordinary maintainer review and CI gating.

target.title: Enable pace prediction for Ollama usage windows
target.body: ## Summary - Set Ollama session windows to 5 hours and weekly windows to 7 days. - Enable session pace rendering for Ollama. - Add regression coverage for Ollama window durations and session pace text. ## Notes Ollama documents that each plan has session limits that reset every 5 hours and weekly limits that reset every 7 days: https://ollama.com/pricing This keeps the session pace allowlist explicit for now by adding Ollama alongside Codex and Claude. Longer term, session pace should probably be generalized the same way weekly pace was, so any provider with an explicit reset time and window duration can use the shared pace calculation without provider- specific gating. ## Screenshot ## Tests - `swift test --filter OllamaUsageParserTests` - `swift test --filter UsagePaceTextTests`
target.author: bdamokos
